title: Lesson 413
transcript: Okay, we are in domain number seven, which as you know, is called security operations. And that is the day-to-day activities that the security function and those that are part of it are participating in on a day-to-day basis, right? So that's what security operations is all about. we're in section 7.14 and what this one deals with as you know there's always value in looking at the exact wording that comes directly from the exam outline. Remember, I know we've repeated this many times, but it's worth repeating because it's good to know. But our entire system, including how we address each of these domains and the material within them, is exactly aligned with the exam outline, which is ultimately where the exam questions come from. So if you look at the exam outline for domain number seven, which is security operations, in section 14, you'll see that it says exactly this. All right, implement and manage physical security, which means the exam is going to try and measure your competence in exactly the wording that you'll see here. So those people that you know, write exam questions for ISE 2 and then those questions go through a series of steps to make them perfect questions to measure your competence Look at the exact wording here because as a competent security professional, you are supposed to be able to implement and manage physical security as part of the security operations of an organization and what the security function obviously does in relation to that. All right, so there's gonna be questions in the exam pane that are obviously focused on measuring your competence level in exactly what you see here. And this of course is true of all of the sections that we're covering in this course, they are completely aligned with the exam outline itself. Now, specifically for this, obviously you need to be able to implement and manage physical security. We've talked about the importance of physical security, right? Because when you're trying to protect assets and do things related to security, You can't just focus on the logical and technical and administrative aspects of security. We also have to focus on physical security. And even though there might be a separate function in the organization called physical security, for example, we have to be able to support the physical security aspects of the organization. And this specific section we've already covered in domain number three. There's obviously overlap between all of the domains, And so to us, it made more sense from a logical perspective to cover physical security in its entirety in domain number three. So domain number three has the physical security things that you need from start to finish at the end of domain number three. That's where we covered it. And all of the material that they can possibly ask you questions on is covered there.
